stories.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire on the Brink: Trump Threatens Renewed Bombing as Peace Talks Stall
stories.summary: A weeks-long ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday, with President Trump warning he is 'ready to go' back to war if no deal is reached. Iran has refused to attend a new round of peace talks in Pakistan, saying it will not negotiate 'under the shadow of threats', while the US maintains a naval blockade of Iranian ports that has shut the Strait of Hormuz to oil tankers. The standoff is sending global oil prices higher and raising fears of a wider conflict in the Middle East.
stories.bigWordDef: A temporary agreement between two sides at war to stop fighting for a set period of time, like pressing 'pause' on a conflict.
stories.thinkQuestion: When two countries disagree on who broke the rules of a ceasefire, how should the rest of the world decide who is right and what should happen next?

stories.headline: Powerful 7.7-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Japan, Raising Fears of a Catastrophic 'Megaquake'
stories.summary: A magnitude 7.7 earthquake struck off Japan's northeastern coast on Monday, triggering tsunami warnings for waves up to 3 metres high before the alert was downgraded and lifted. With no casualties reported, Japan's government issued a rare 'megaquake advisory' warning that the probability of an even larger magnitude-8+ earthquake striking in the next week is 10 times higher than normal — about 1%. Residents across 182 towns from Hokkaido to Chiba have been urged to confirm their evacuation plans.
stories.bigWordDef: Relating to earthquakes or the vibrations and energy that travel through the Earth when the ground shakes.
stories.thinkQuestion: Japan sits on the 'Ring of Fire' and experiences earthquakes constantly — what can a country do to prepare its citizens for disasters that scientists can warn about but cannot predict exactly?
stories.videoQuery: Japan earthquake megaquake Ring of Fire explained

stories.headline: At Least 25 Killed in Tamil Nadu Firecracker Factory Blast in Virudhunagar
stories.summary: A devastating explosion tore through the Vanaja fireworks factory in Kattanarpatti village, Virudhunagar district, on Sunday afternoon, killing at least 25 workers — many of them women — and injuring several others. The blast, one of the deadliest industrial accidents in the region in years, is believed to have been triggered by friction during the handling of explosive raw materials; the factory was reportedly operating on a Sunday when the industry is officially shut. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin ordered a high-level probe, while critics pointed to 262 deaths in the district's fireworks industry over the past decade.
stories.bigWordDef: Failing to take proper care or follow safety rules, especially when that failure causes harm to others.
stories.thinkQuestion: Virudhunagar is called the 'fireworks capital of India' and provides livelihoods to thousands of workers — how should governments balance protecting workers' safety with preserving their jobs in dangerous industries?

stories.headline: Karnataka Becomes First Indian State to Ban Social Media for Children Under 16
stories.summary: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced in the state's 2026–27 budget that social media will be banned for all children under the age of 16, making Karnataka the first Indian state to take this step. The decision follows Australia's nationwide ban and mirrors growing global concern about digital addiction, cyberbullying, and deteriorating mental health among young people; a 2025 survey found Indian teenagers spend over 4.5 hours daily on screens. Critics and digital rights groups have raised questions about how the ban will be enforced and whether it falls within a state's legal authority, since internet regulation is generally a central government matter in India.
stories.bigWordDef: The official right or power of an authority (like a state government) to make and enforce laws in a particular area or on a particular subject.
stories.thinkQuestion: Should governments have the power to decide which apps or websites people of different ages can use, or should that decision be left to parents and families?
stories.videoQuery: social media ban children India Karnataka explained

stories.headline: Emperor Penguin and Antarctic Fur Seal Classified as 'Endangered' as Climate Change Shrinks Sea Ice
stories.summary: The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has downgraded both the emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal from safer categories to 'Endangered' on its Red List, citing climate change as the primary reason. The emperor penguin's population is projected to halve by the 2080s as warming oceans destroy the sea ice it needs to breed, while the Antarctic fur seal has already lost nearly 50% of its population since 1999 due to reduced food availability. Scientists warn these two species serve as vital early-warning signals for the health of the entire Antarctic ecosystem.
stories.bigWordDef: All the living things (animals, plants, bacteria) in a particular area and the way they interact with each other and their environment.
stories.thinkQuestion: If the emperor penguin goes extinct, it would affect the entire food chain in Antarctica — what does that tell us about why protecting even one species matters for the whole planet?
stories.videoQuery: emperor penguin endangered climate change Antarctic ice

stories.headline: IPL 2026: CSK Beat KKR by 32 Runs; RCB vs LSG Battle Set for Bengaluru Tonight
stories.summary: Chennai Super Kings defeated Kolkata Knight Riders by 32 runs at Chepauk on April 14, recording back-to-back wins for the first time in two years. Afghan spinner Noor Ahmad was the star with the ball, claiming 3 wickets for just 21 runs, while Sanju Samson (48), Dewald Brevis (41) and teenager Ayush Mhatre (38 off 17 balls) powered CSK to 192. KKR remain winless this season, and tonight the attention turns to Bengaluru's M. Chinnaswamy Stadium where defending champions RCB host Lucknow Super Giants.
stories.bigWordDef: The first 6 overs of a T20 innings when fielding restrictions apply, meaning only two fielders are allowed outside the inner circle — making it easier for batters to hit boundaries.
stories.thinkQuestion: CSK's Noor Ahmad is from Afghanistan and plays for an Indian Premier League team — what does the IPL tell us about the way sport can bring together talent from different countries?
stories.videoQuery: IPL 2026 highlights CSK KKR Noor Ahmad

videos.title: Why Japan Gets So Many Earthquakes — The Ring of Fire Explained
videos.description: A clear explainer on why Japan sits at the meeting point of tectonic plates and what a 'megaquake' advisory actually means for millions of people.

videos.title: Emperor Penguins: Racing Against Melting Ice
videos.description: How climate change is destroying Antarctic sea ice and pushing the emperor penguin — the world's largest penguin — towards extinction.

videos.title: The Strait of Hormuz: Why This Waterway Controls the World's Oil
videos.description: An accessible geography explainer on why the narrow Strait of Hormuz between Iran and Oman is one of the most strategically important shipping lanes on Earth.

infographic.caption: Today's World in Numbers — April 21, 2026
infographic.fact: Japan accounts for ~20% of all magnitude-6+ earthquakes worldwide · The Strait of Hormuz carries roughly 20–25% of global oil supply · At least 262 workers killed in Virudhunagar fireworks accidents since 2010 · Emperor penguin population projected to halve by the 2080s · Indian teenagers now spend an average of 4.5 hours daily on screens
infographic.question: Looking at today's stories, which single issue do you think will have the biggest impact on your life in 20 years — and why?
